per_criterion.rationale: Openings are mixed. Sharp-01 leads with 'OpenAI's B2B Signals gives enterprises the language they needed' — named entity + specific number (6) anchors first-fixation, and the body closes the gap with the audit framing. Sharp-02 uses an antithesis hook ('We use AI tells you nothing... 4 of 6 operational areas') that opens a specific bounded gap and delivers within the post. But Build-01's 'B2B Signals is the scoreboard enterprises didn't know they needed' veers into topic-statement framing, and Build-02's 'The way agencies price AI transformation is broken' is a meta-frame the body unpacks via padded Before/Now lists. Case-study-01 has a working narrative hook (founder quote) but the body devolves into a 6-item enumeration of OpenAI's framework rather than instantiating the founder anecdote tightly. 003-audit's hook 'Most agencies audit digital maturity by counting tool SKUs' works as a peer-targeted contrarian opening. 004-vaporware's 'AI costume department' line is schema-violating but the lead 'Your client says they're using AI' is a generic setup. Cohort hook quality averages to mid — multiple drafts pass Axes A/B but slip on Axis C (hook-body alignment dilutes through enumeration).

evidence.quote: OpenAI's B2B Signals gives enterprises the language they needed: "which of 6 key workflows are agentic" instead of vague "we use AI."
"We use AI" tells you nothing. "We use agentic workflows in 4 of 6 operational areas" tells you everything.
B2B Signals is the scoreboard enterprises didn't know they needed.
The way agencies price AI transformation is broken.
Most agencies audit "digital maturity" by counting tool SKUs.

per_criterion.rationale: PRE-REQ gate fires: programs/references/voice.md is NOT present in source_data — the artifact bundle includes angles/*.json, drafts/, findings.md, report.md, but no voice substrate file. The drafts make several first-person lived-work claims naming entities ('gofreddy audit framework', 'autoresearch workflow maturity audits', 'I sat across from a B2B SaaS founder', 'gofreddy SaaS engagement', 'gofreddy lens catalog v2', 'PR #28, #33, #34') that the judge cannot verify against ground truth. Per the rubric, when voice.md is absent or empty in source_data, emit 0.5/unknown for the whole criterion rather than HARD FLOOR or default-pass. The drafts' authority_anchors look plausibly grounded in JR's own work but cannot be checked against the substrate.

evidence.quote: I started using B2B Signals in gofreddy audits across 11 marketing areas.
Last month I sat across from a B2B SaaS founder who said "we're AI-native now. Claude does our content, GPT our SEO analysis, we ship twice as fast."
attribution: https://openai.com/index/introducing-b2b-signals + gofreddy SaaS engagement

per_criterion.rationale: Most drafts make claims a thoughtful peer could substantively disagree with — e.g., that B2B Signals (an OpenAI marketing piece) is genuinely a useful scoring framework rather than vendor PR; that '0/6 agentic' is a fair scorecard for a team running Claude + GPT-assisted workflows; that pricing $12k/workflow-tier is a defensible repricing model. Peers could counter that humans-with-AI workflows ARE agentic enough; that OpenAI's framework is self-serving rather than neutral; that the audit-the-CRM playbook ignores legitimate constraints. But several drafts hedge into manufactured-controversy territory — 005-case-study's '0/5 agentic' scores for a team that has built signal detection are provocations more than substantive claims, and build-02's pricing arithmetic ($40k strategy → $12k per tier) is presented without justification a peer could engage. Falsifiability present but uneven across the cohort.

evidence.quote: Turns out, most enterprises "use AI" in 2-3 workflows max. The other 9 are humans doing AI tasks.

Before: $40k strategy + undefined scope
Now: $12k per workflow tier (1 to 4 to 6)
This founder scored 0/6. They licensed tools. They didn't automate workflows.

per_criterion.rationale: Form mostly matches density. Sharp drafts (256-278 chars) carry one coherent claim each and resolve within the post. Build drafts (654-728 chars) layer one frame with bulleted support — borderline padded but each bullet reveals a distinct angle. Case-study-01 (1247 chars) earns its length via narrative scaffolding (founder quote → diagnosis → 6-area framework → pitch reframe), though the 6-item enumeration mid-post breaks the case-study rhythm and reads as inserted framework restating rather than per-unit revelation. 005-case-study (1247 chars) walks Week 1/Week 2/Week 3 narrative with distinct scores per area — earns its length better. 004-vaporware's 5-bullet enumeration restates the same diagnostic across rows (Content/Sales/Retention/Conversion/Attribution) without much new revelation per bullet. Cohort form-fit is mid: form is appropriate, but bullet density inside long-form drafts dilutes per-unit revelation.

evidence.quote:
1. Is content triggered by signal/schedule or human decision?
2. Does paid optimization happen in real-time or in batches?
3. Are leads routed + prioritized by agent logic?
4. Does lifecycle messaging adapt to individual behavior or run fixed campaigns?
5. Is attribution modeled continuously or quarterly?
6. Are roadmap priorities driven by data synthesis or gut?
**Week 1: Content.**

per_criterion.rationale: Cold-start regime — no prior posts visible in source_data, and the bio/positioning file (voice.md) is also absent, so positioning-consistency check fires partial-unknown. AI-slop stack check: drafts mostly avoid em-dash density (a few present but sparse), no 'Stop X. Start Y.' imperative-pairs, but Build-02 stacks parallel 'Before:/Now:' rhythm 3 times consecutively — that's a Tier-2 listicle-parallelism tell. 004-vaporware uses reflexive parallel construction across all 5 bullets ('Are they generating... / Does AI route... / Do at-risk signals... / Is funnel optimization... / Can they trace...') which approaches the parallel-listicle slop signature. Jargon-gloss check fails repeatedly: 'agentic workflows', 'ICP scoring', 'GTM', 'ARR', 'NRR', 'compounding' all appear without inline plain-English context — JR's stated voice anchor is accessibility-to-non-engineer-founder/marketer, and these drafts assume in-niche vocabulary throughout. Tone is operator-peer-not-mentor in some drafts (sharp ones) but lesson-extracting/conclusive in others ('This framework turns I think we need AI into our Content and Paid are the leverage points' — broadcast register). Mixed.

evidence.quote:
Before: "Your content process needs AI."
Now: "You're at 1/6 on content workflows. I'll get you to 4/6 in 12 weeks."

Before: $40k strategy + undefined scope
Now: $12k per workflow tier (1 to 4 to 6)

Before: "AI adoption" (vague positioning)
Now: "Workflow maturity tracking" (measurement)

→ Content: Are they generating weekly or monthly? 
→ Sales: Does AI route leads or does a junior SDR? 
→ Retention: Do at-risk signals trigger workflows or silence?
→ Conversion: Is funnel optimization algorithmic or manual retargeting?
→ Attribution: Can they trace ROI through the agent stack or is it guesswork?

per_criterion.rationale: Cohort is concentrated, not spread. Multiple drafts (build-01, build-02, case-study-01, 003-audit, 004-vaporware, 005-case-study) all lean on the same primary differentiator: OpenAI's '6 operational areas / N-of-6 scoring' framework. Build-01 and Build-02 both pitch agency-pricing/positioning shifts via the same scorecard mechanic. Both case studies (case-study-01 and 005-case-study) use the same hook archetype: 'I audited a client → they scored low on agentic workflows → here's the pitch.' Sharp-01 and Sharp-02 are both contrarian antithesis hooks targeting the 'we use AI' vague-claim pattern. Voice pillar collapses to 'AI-native marketing' across 7 of 8 drafts (only build-02 declares 'operator-efficiency'). Source citations all chain to the same openai.com/b2b-signals URL. Hooks include scoreboard/diagnostic/audit/pricing/vaporware framings — moderate hook variety but the underlying mental model is identical. A reader scanning the cohort would see variations on one thesis, not 8 distinct angles. Per the rubric's geometric-mean approach to cohort-fit, this scores closer to 1 (same differentiator, same source, same framework) than 5.

evidence.quote: OpenAI's B2B Signals gives enterprises the language they needed: "which of 6 key workflows are agentic"
"We use agentic workflows in 4 of 6 operational areas" tells you everything.
OpenAI's framework maps 6 operational areas where work either compounds or stays manual.
The framework names 6 operational areas where workflows either are or aren't agentic

per_criterion.rationale: All angle-121 drafts pass X-9. Sub-question 1 (BODY URL-free): no [BODY] block contains http://, https://, or bare-domain forms in any of the 8 angle-121 drafts — URLs appear only inside [META] blocks (sharp-01, sharp-02, build-01, build-02, case-study-01) which is metadata, not the publish body. Sub-question 2 (REPLY URL-free): only one angle-121 draft has a [REPLY] block (case-study-01) and it contains no URL — just 'For anyone selling AI transformation to enterprises: you need this framework. Go read it. Changes how you diagnosis.' Sub-question 3 (named-source citation pattern): drafts handle source attribution by naming inline — 'OpenAI's B2B Signals research', 'OpenAI just handed agencies', 'OpenAI's framework maps' — without embedding the link. Sub-question 4 (no disguised links): no 'link in bio', 'DM for the PDF', shortened URLs, or QR references in any body or reply. Algorithmic citizenship pitfall fully avoided.

primary.notes: Voice substrate (programs/references/voice.md) is absent from source_data, so X-2 falls to the cold-start PRE-REQ unknown band (0.5). Cohort suffers from framework-monoculture: 6 of 8 angle-121 drafts pivot on OpenAI's 'N-of-6 operational areas' scoring, collapsing X-6 diversity. Hook quality is mixed — sharp drafts work, build/case-study drafts tilt toward framework-enumeration. Jargon (ICP, GTM, ARR, agentic, compounding) appears unglossed throughout, breaking the stated accessibility-to-non-engineer-founder contract and capping X-5. Algorithmic-citizenship pitfall (X-9) is cleanly avoided — no external URLs in any BODY or REPLY block. Note: the session_summary.txt claims angle 121 has 4 drafts (jr-2026-05-12-001..004) but those filenames in drafts/ are actually tagged angle_id 122 (CRM); the real angle-121 drafts are the b2b-signals-* + 003-audit + 004-vaporware + 005-case-study files. Scored against the actual angle-121-tagged drafts.

per_criterion.rationale: Most visible angle-121 openings are first-fixation survivable: they anchor on named entities/numbers like OpenAI, B2B Signals, and 6 workflows, then close the promised gap with workflow/audit language. The stronger drafts avoid throat-clearing and deliver the bounded distinction between vague AI adoption and agentic workflow maturity.

evidence.quote: "We use AI" tells you nothing. "We use agentic workflows in 4 of 6 operational areas" tells you everything.
